channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A provocative new film takes a hard look at Israeli life during the Gaza war
item.description: The Israeli film Yes criticizes Israeli artists during the Gaza war, and stars one of Israel's most provocative fringe artists, Ariel Bronz.
item.content:encoded: The Israeli film Yes criticizes Israeli artists during the Gaza war, and stars one of Israel's most provocative fringe artists, Ariel Bronz.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Progress on DHS funding stalls as polarized public opinion becomes more entrenched
item.description: Public support for Democrats and Republicans has hardened in recent months, leaving an increasingly narrow sliver of undecided voters.
item.content:encoded: Public support for Democrats and Republicans has hardened in recent months, leaving an increasingly narrow sliver of undecided voters.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro

item.title: Hegseth blocked 2 Black and 2 female soldiers from promotions
item.description: Pete Hegseth has reportedly blocked the military promotion of six officers: all women or Black men, to become one-star generals.
item.content:encoded: Pete Hegseth has reportedly blocked the military promotion of six officers: all women or Black men, to become one-star generals.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence

item.title: Jigsaw puzzling is a growing competitive activity — with a national tournament
item.description: There's a big national competition in Atlanta this week in puzzling. Teams will race to put jigsaw pieces into place, assembling 500 and 1,000 piece puzzles they've never seen.
item.content:encoded: There's a big national competition in Atlanta this week in puzzling. Teams will race to put jigsaw pieces into place, assembling 500 and 1,000 piece puzzles they've never seen.
item.dc:creator: Leslie E. Thompson

item.title: Polymarket tries to woo a skeptical D.C. with a prediction market-themed bar
item.description: One of the largest prediction market platforms, Polymarket, opened a pop-up bar in the heart of Washington, D.C.'s lobbying district.
item.content:encoded: One of the largest prediction market platforms, Polymarket, opened a pop-up bar in the heart of Washington, D.C.'s lobbying district.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett

item.title: Courtney Barnett returns with 'Creature of Habit'
item.description: Singer-songwriter Courtney Barnett's latest album is a reflection on a changed woman. Our New Music Friday discussion breaks it down.
item.content:encoded: Singer-songwriter Courtney Barnett's latest album is a reflection on a changed woman. Our New Music Friday discussion breaks it down.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levitt

item.title: From Tehran to Tel Aviv, the Gulf and Beirut: Regional reflections on one month of war
item.description: One month into the U.S.–Israeli war with Iran, we turn to our correspondents across the region to assess the conflict's impact.
item.content:encoded: One month into the U.S.–Israeli war with Iran, we turn to our correspondents across the region to assess the conflict's impact.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: 2 games for the U.S. men's soccer team are a preview of their chances in the World Cup
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team is set to host two World Cup contenders, Belgium and Portugal, in blockbuster friendly matchups that will show whether the Americans have a chance at success.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. men's national soccer team is set to host two World Cup contenders, Belgium and Portugal, in blockbuster friendly matchups that will show whether the Americans have a chance at success.
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: House Republicans reject Senate plan to end DHS shutdown
item.description: A Senate bill to end the shutdown at the Department of Homeland Security is facing stiff resistance in the House, leaving funding for the agency in doubt.
item.content:encoded: A Senate bill to end the shutdown at the Department of Homeland Security is facing stiff resistance in the House, leaving funding for the agency in doubt.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: Cuban Americans face scrutiny for sending goods back to the island
item.description: Cuban Americans who ship goods to relatives on the island are now seen as propping up Cuba's communist regime as the economy there continues to deteriorate.
item.content:encoded: Cuban Americans who ship goods to relatives on the island are now seen as propping up Cuba's communist regime as the economy there continues to deteriorate.
item.dc:creator: David Ovalle
